questions.question: What is Whisper's basic architecture and input contract?
options: A decoder-only LLM over raw waveforms

A transformer encoder-decoder pinned to a 30-second window: 80-mel log spectrogram at 10ms hop (3000 frames), conv-downsampled into the encoder; the decoder cross-attends and emits BPE tokens over a 51,865-token vocab
A CNN classifier with a CTC head
An RNN-T with a streaming joiner
questions.explanation: Shorter clips are zero-padded, longer ones chunked. Large-v3 is 1.55B params (32 layers, 1280-dim); everything in the pipeline is pinned to that 30-second window.

questions.question: How does one Whisper model do transcription, translation, and 99 languages?
options: 99 separate fine-tuned checkpoints

Special tokens in the decoder prompt steer the task: <|en|> sets language, <|transcribe|> vs <|translate|> picks the task, <|notimestamps|> skips timestamps — change <|en|> to <|fr|> and it transcribes French
An external language detector reroutes audio
Language-specific encoders share a decoder
questions.explanation: Whisper is a multitask model steered by its prompt format — the same mechanism that later VLMs and omni models reuse for task routing.

questions.question: What did the Turbo variant change, and what did it cost?
options: It quantized weights to 4-bit
It cut the decoder from 32 layers to 4, reducing latency 8x with under 1% WER hit (1.58% on LibriSpeech-clean at 809M params vs Large-v3's 1.8% at 1.55B)
It shrank the encoder to 2 layers
It dropped multilingual support
questions.explanation: The asymmetry is the lesson: most of Whisper's intelligence lives in the encoder; the decoder is mostly rendering text, so it can be radically shrunk.

questions.question: Why must you use Whisper's own preprocessing instead of librosa's mel spectrogram?
options: librosa is too slow for production
Whisper normalizes log-mels with mean/std statistics from ITS OWN training corpus — mismatched preprocessing silently degrades output even though shapes and code all work
librosa outputs a different dtype
Licensing forbids mixing libraries
questions.explanation: A classic silent failure: whisper.audio.log_mel_spectrogram bakes in the training-time convention. This is the Phase 6 theme — convention violations don't crash, they just quietly double your WER.

questions.question: When does Whisper need help (chunking tools or fine-tuning)?
options: Never — it's robust to everything

Domain shift kills it: technical jargon, accents, proper nouns, short clips, and silence; long-form and streaming need external tooling (WhisperX, Whisper-Streaming, faster-whisper) because the 30s window isn't natively streamed
Only on non-English audio
Only when the GPU has under 8GB
questions.explanation: Whisper ships as a commodity but isn't a black box forever — knowing what's inside tells you when to chunk differently, when to prompt, and when to fine-tune.
